message:
feat(frankenpandas): expose 5 index-cols reader variants (fd90.260 / br-h9tbf)

Five fp-io readers were missing from the frankenpandas top-level
re-export (kept out of the prelude — these are more specialized than
the basic variants already there):

- read_csv_with_index_cols / _path — match pandas
  pd.read_csv(..., index_col=[0, 1]) (multi-column index promotion)
- read_sql_with_index_col / read_sql_table_with_index_col — single
  index_col promotion at SQL read time
- read_sql_table_columns — list columns from a SQL table

User code with `use frankenpandas::read_csv_with_index_cols;` couldn't
resolve these names before this commit.

Verified: 36 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-h6ji","title":"CONFORMANCE: pandas DataFrame parity matrix","description":"Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses skill Pattern 4: edge-case matrix for the DataFrame family. Sibling to the Series matrix (br-3qt8 shipped).

Ops × edge inputs:
- dataframe_sum (aggregation axis=0): empty frame, single-row, all-NaN column, mixed-dtype columns, NaN-heavy
- dataframe_head: n=0 on nonempty, n > len, negative-n (head(-1) returns all-but-last)
- dataframe_count: empty frame, all-NaN

Test file: crates/fp-conformance/src/tests/conformance_dataframe.rs registered in lib.rs. Each test constructs a PacketFixture with oracle_source=live_legacy_pandas, captures the oracle, builds the frame via build_fixture_frame, dispatches the Rust op, and compares via compare_frame_expected / compare_scalar.

Target: 8 tests, all green. Any divergence → separate [HIGH] review bead.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-24T22:24:07.622928289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T22:26:18.706566486Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T22:26:18.703985589Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","dataframe","parity-matrix","review"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-h8a8","title":"[MEDIUM] crates.io: 11 of 12 Cargo.toml carry no description/keywords/categories/repository — release-day discoverability gap","description":"crates.io metadata audit.

**Observation.** Out of 12 Cargo.toml files in the workspace, ZERO carry `keywords`, `categories`, `repository`, `homepage`, `documentation`, or `readme`. Only the umbrella `frankenpandas` crate has a `description` field; the 11 fp-* crates have none.

**Impact at crates.io release (br-4clx prerequisite).**
- `description`: crates.io shows "(no description given)" on search results + crate pages. Users scrolling browse results skip.
- `keywords`: crates.io keyword search (e.g. "dataframe", "pandas", "analytics") misses the crate. Up to 5 keywords per crate; each is a free discoverability channel.
- `categories`: crates.io has a curated category hierarchy (database::implementations, data-structures, science, etc.). Without it, the crate doesn't appear in any category browse.
- `repository`: no "Source" button on the crate page. Users can't jump from crates.io to GitHub.
- `homepage`: separate from repository — for projects with a dedicated website (frankensuite-website-development skill implies one exists).
- `documentation`: docs.rs auto-links via crate name so this is non-blocking, but setting it explicitly lets us point to a custom hosted docs if we ever diverge from docs.rs.
- `readme`: per-crate README pointer. By default it auto-detects but it's the kind of thing that silently breaks after a refactor.

**Severity.** MEDIUM — release-day discoverability gap. Not a correctness issue; not a data-safety issue; not a performance issue. Honest calibration: fixing after release means users who adopted 0.1.0 see outdated metadata until 0.1.1 ships; fixing before release is free.

**Fix shape.**

1. Workspace-level `[workspace.package]` gets a shared baseline:
   ```toml
   [workspace.package]
   edition = "2024"
   version = "0.1.0"
   license-file = "LICENSE"
   repository = "https://github.com/<org>/frankenpandas"
   homepage = "https://frankenpandas.dev"          # if the site exists
   documentation = "https://docs.rs/frankenpandas"
   readme = "README.md"
   keywords = ["dataframe", "pandas", "analytics", "rust", "columnar"]
   categories = ["database-implementations", "data-structures", "science"]
   ```

2. Each fp-* Cargo.toml [package] inherits via `.workspace = true`:
   ```toml
   [package]
   name = "fp-frame"
   version.workspace = true
   edition.workspace = true
   repository.workspace = true
   homepage.workspace = true
   documentation.workspace = true
   readme.workspace = true
   keywords.workspace = true
   categories.workspace = true
   license-file.workspace = true
   description = "DataFrame and Series core for the frankenpandas workspace."
   ```

   Per-crate description remains bespoke (each fp-* crate does a different thing; workspace-level description would be generic). Keywords can be workspace-inherited because the discoverability terms overlap across crates.

3. Per-crate descriptions to draft (each ≤ 200 chars per crates.io limit):
   - fp-types: "Core Scalar, DType, NullKind primitives for the frankenpandas workspace — the universal type system behind fp-frame."
   - fp-columnar: "Columnar storage primitives (Column, ValidityMask) for pandas-parity dataframes."
   - fp-index: "Flat and multi-level row index types (Index, MultiIndex) for pandas-parity dataframes."
   - fp-runtime: "Runtime policies, evidence ledgers, and asupersync interop for the frankenpandas execution layer."
   - fp-frame: "DataFrame and Series with pandas-API parity and AACE index alignment."
   - fp-io: "IO layer: CSV, JSON, Parquet, Excel, Feather, Arrow IPC, SQL (SQLite; PostgreSQL/MySQL planned)."
   - fp-groupby: "GroupBy engine for fp-frame with dense / arena / hash-map execution paths."
   - fp-join: "Merge and join engine (AG-05 leapfrog triejoin) for fp-frame DataFrames."
   - fp-expr: "Expression eval / query parser for fp-frame DataFrames (pandas df.eval/df.query parity)."
   - fp-conformance: "Conformance harness: differential test suite against live pandas oracle."
   - fp-frankentui: "FrankenTUI surface for interactive fp-frame workflows."

4. Categories per crate: mostly shared ("data-structures", "science") but IO crate can claim "filesystem" / "database-implementations"; runtime crate can claim "concurrency"; groupby/join can claim "algorithms".

Blast radius: workspace Cargo.toml + 11 per-crate Cargo.toml edits (~5-10 line additions each). Zero source changes. Per-crate descriptions need proofreading (~1 min each).

Prerequisites:
- br-4clx (release workflow) — this metadata is discovered at publish time; landing this bead BEFORE 4clx's first cargo publish saves a 0.1.1 patch.

**Related:**
- br-4clx (release + workspace version inheritance) — same commit wave; natural pairing.
- br-60du (CHANGELOG stale) — another release-day metadata bead.
- br-wskz (docs.rs metadata) — companion: wskz controls docs.rs build config; this bead controls crates.io page content.
- br-8k1i (SECURITY.md) — repository URL in Cargo.toml points at where SECURITY.md lives.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:51:51.435830680Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-23T16:29:03.097603234Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T16:29:03.095090202Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crates-io","metadata","release","review"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-h950j","title":"fd90.225: Cover DataFrame.{eq,ne,gt,lt,le,ge}_scalar_df shortcuts","description":"fd90.225: Cover DataFrame.{eq,ne,gt,lt,le,ge}_scalar_df shortcut methods.

Same pattern as fd90.224 (Series shortcuts) — DataFrame has 6 scalar comparison shortcut methods at fp-frame:24814-24839 with zero integration coverage. The compare_scalar_df dispatch form is exercised in fd90.223; the *_scalar_df shortcuts are the ergonomic alternative.

Scope:
- Extend readme_element_wise_operations_compiles_and_runs with all 6 calls.

Files: crates/frankenpandas/tests/readme_quick_example.rs.
Time estimate: 10-15min.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"test","created_at":"2026-04-27T03:07:11.214780059Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T03:09:01.710179793Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T03:09:01.708081350Z","close_reason":"Extended readme_element_wise_operations_compiles_and_runs to cover all 6 DataFrame scalar comparison shortcut methods: eq_scalar_df, ne_scalar_df, gt_scalar_df, lt_scalar_df, le_scalar_df, ge_scalar_df. Pairs with fd90.223 (compare_scalar_df dispatch) and fd90.224 (Series shortcuts). All 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-h9tbf","title":"fd90.260: Expose CSV/SQL index-cols reader variants to top-level","description":"fd90.260: Expose CSV/SQL index-cols reader variants to top-level.

Five fp-io readers are not exposed in frankenpandas:
- read_csv_with_index_cols (line 1141) — string + multi-column index promotion
- read_csv_with_index_cols_path (line 1164) — path variant
- read_sql_with_index_col — single index column
- read_sql_table_with_index_col — table-based with index_col
- read_sql_table_columns — list columns from a SQL table

These match pandas pd.read_csv(..., index_col=[0, 1]) and pd.read_sql_table(..., index_col=...) patterns.

Scope (keep tight):
- Add 5 readers to top-level fp_io re-export
- Don't add to prelude (more specialized than the basic variants already in prelude)

Files: crates/frankenpandas/src/lib.rs.
Time estimate: 10-15min.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T07:41:57.160643785Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T07:44:02.422838266Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T07:44:02.420815595Z","close_reason":"Exposed 5 fp-io readers at the top-level frankenpandas re-export: read_csv_with_index_cols (string + multi-column index promotion), read_csv_with_index_cols_path (path variant), read_sql_with_index_col, read_sql_table_with_index_col, read_sql_table_columns (list columns from a SQL table). Match pandas pd.read_csv(..., index_col=[0,1]) and pd.read_sql_table(..., index_col=...) patterns. Kept out of the prelude (more specialized than the basic variants already there). All 36 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-hb9xg","title":"fd90.140: README FrameError row lists dead DuplicateIndexUnsupported (never constructed); should show real wrappers","description":"fd90.74-139 README audit. Error Architecture table at line 826 lists FrameError::DuplicateIndexUnsupported as a 'Key Variant':
  | FrameError | fp-frame | LengthMismatch { index_len, column_len }, DuplicateIndexUnsupported, CompatibilityRejected(String) |

But the variant is declared at crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:113 and NEVER CONSTRUCTED anywhere in the workspace. Verified with:
  grep -rnE 'DuplicateIndexUnsupported\b' crates/  →  only the declaration, no usages

The error message ('duplicate index labels are unsupported in strict mode for MVP slice') suggests it was reserved for future strict-mode work that hasn't materialized. Listing it as 'key variant' that users will pattern-match is misleading — they'd never see it returned today.

The enum is #[non_exhaustive] so it could be removed from code, but the post-MVP intent makes that risky to preempt. Cleaner to update the README to show variants users actually will encounter:

Real variants users see today:
- LengthMismatch { index_len, column_len }
- CompatibilityRejected(String)  -- the catch-all returned by most validation failures
- Column(ColumnError)             -- transparent wrapper from #[from] ColumnError
- Index(IndexError)               -- transparent wrapper from #[from] IndexError

Fix: drop DuplicateIndexUnsupported from the FrameError row; add the two transparent wrappers (Column, Index) which users WILL match against. Pure README change.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T13:17:16.920538982Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T13:18:36.985662713Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T13:18:36.982916746Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-he7yj","title":"fd90.233: Add direct df.min/max/std/var/median/prod/skew/kurt/sem methods (parity)","description":"fd90.233: DataFrame is missing direct min/max/std/var/median/prod/skew/kurtosis/sem reduction methods (pandas parity gap).

Discovered while writing fd90.232: DataFrame has 7 direct reduction methods (sum, mean, count, nunique, abs, all, any) but is missing the rest of the pandas-equivalent surface:

Pandas has:
- df.min() / df.max() — per-column min/max as Series
- df.std() / df.var() — per-column std/var (ddof=1 default)
- df.median() — per-column median
- df.prod() — per-column product
- df.skew() / df.kurt() / df.kurtosis() — higher-order moments
- df.sem() — standard error of mean

In fp-frame, these are reachable per-column (iterate columns, call Series.min()/etc.) or via df.describe() which produces them as a matrix. Direct DataFrame methods would be the natural pandas-parity ergonomic.

Note: df.min() / df.max() at fp-frame:12142/12147 LOOK like DataFrame methods but are actually inside `impl DatetimeAccessor`. The Iterator::min() trait fallback also fails to compile cleanly on `df.min()`.

This is a pandas-parity feature gap, not a regression. Filing for tracking; not for immediate implementation since the workaround (via columns or describe) exists.

Scope (when implemented):
- Add 9 reduction methods to `impl DataFrame {` returning per-column Series, matching the existing sum/mean shape.
- Match pandas defaults (ddof=1 for std/var/sem; numeric_only auto-detection).
- Add integration test coverage.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-27T04:37:14.665439971Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T04:50:02.849455011Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T04:50:02.847405529Z","close_reason":"Added 10 pandas-parity bare-name reduction methods to impl DataFrame: min, max, std, var, median, prod, skew, kurt, kurtosis (alias of kurt), sem. Each is a one-line delegation to the existing *_agg method (min_agg/max_agg/etc. were named _agg-suffixed because of an earlier name-resolution issue, but inherent methods shadow the trait fallback). The existing _agg method docstrings already claimed pandas parity ('Matches pd.DataFrame.min()'), but the user-facing names didn't match — fd90.233 closes that gap. Extended readme_element_wise_operations_compiles_and_runs to exercise all 10. All 1434 fp-frame lib tests + 35 integration + 7 frankenpandas lib + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
